items.myth: Claude is a single model with one fixed personality and knowledge.
items.reality: Claude is a family of models (different tiers and versions) behind a consistent assistant experience. Capabilities, speed, and cost vary by model; apps may default to one tier while APIs let you choose explicitly.
items.example: An API call names `claude-sonnet-…` or `claude-opus-…`; a chat user may see only "Claude" in the UI while the backend selects a model.

items.myth: Claude runs entirely on your device like offline software.
items.reality: Standard Claude chat and API usage send your prompts to Anthropic's (or a partner provider's) cloud infrastructure. Local-only inference is not the default Claude product path.
items.example: claude.ai works in a browser precisely because compute runs remotely—you need a network connection for typical use.

items.myth: Claude "understands" like a human and always knows current facts.
items.reality: Claude is a statistical language model optimized for helpful text. It can be wrong, outdated, or overconfident without retrieval, tools, or human verification—especially on niche or time-sensitive facts.
items.example: Asking for today's stock price without web search may yield a plausible but incorrect number.
